reasoning: The Lean formalization faithfully captures Munkres Exercise 28.5. The custom `countably_compact` definition correctly states that every countable open cover (∀ U : ℕ → Set X with all U i open and covering univ) admits a finite subcover (Finset ℕ indexing). The iff's right-hand side correctly encodes "every nested sequence of closed nonempty sets has nonempty intersection": `IsClosed (C n)` captures closed, `C n ≠ ∅` captures nonempty, `C (n+1) ⊆ C n` captures nestedness (matching Munkres's non-strict ⊃), and `∃ x, ∀ n, x ∈ C n` is equivalent to ⋂ C n ≠ ∅. Re-indexing from C₁ to C 0 is immaterial. The quantifier structure and logical content match exactly.
